title: Protocol Buffers
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries Protocol Buffers content across its three common forms: binary-encoded wire format (`.pb`), the generic binary alias (`.protobuf`), and schema definition language source (`.proto`). Protocol Buffers is Google's language-neutral serialization format used extensively in gRPC services and data pipelines.

## When to use
Use `media:protobuf` when a pipeline node serializes, deserializes, or analyzes protobuf content — for example, a gRPC message encoder/decoder, a schema registry step, or a code generation node that runs `protoc` on `.proto` definitions.

## Notes
Binary `.pb` files are not human-readable; use `.proto` schema files for text-based analysis. The `proto3` syntax is the current standard; `proto2` files remain in use in legacy services. Code generation via `protoc` and language-specific plugins produces stubs for Go, Python, Java, and other languages. See also [Protocol Buffers](https://capdag.com/browse/media:protobuf).
